behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction explicitly names all file paths, field names, constructor lines, and property implementations that tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md provides the exact fix verbatim: field declarations like 'private readonly Lazy<V143Network> network', constructor lines, and property implementations 'public override DevTools.Network Network => this.network.Value' — no reasoning required.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact string matches like 'this.network = new Lazy<V143Network>(() => new V143Network(domains.Network, domains.Fetch))' and 'public override DevTools.Network Network => this.network.Value'; alternative correct implementations (e.g., different field names, Lazy initialization in field initializer, different whitespace) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most f2p tests are substring checks on file content (e.g., assert 'private readonly Lazy<V143Network> network' in content); only the bazel build test executes code, and it doesn't verify the caching behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub wouldn't match the specific literal strings asserted, so tests require the specific implementation.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover all three files and all four properties plus the test file existence as described in instruction.md.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments 'Fail-to-pass tests' and 'Pass-to-pass tests' with descriptive function names.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and identifiers are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names exact file paths and specifies exact code to write verbatim, turning this into a transcription exercise rather than a bug localization/fix task.
